content.text: Now let me check if other stories have been updated with the correct Mantine Select interaction pattern:

input.description: Find all selectOptions and listbox usage in stories

content.text: I can see the correct pattern in `ResponsesForm.stories.tsx`. Let me also check the `NewPlayerForm` story:

content.text:
Both stories need updating. The pattern from `ResponsesForm.stories.tsx` is:
1. Click the combobox to open the dropdown
2. Find the listbox in `document.body`
3. Click the option by `role="option"`

Let me fix both stories:

result.output: The file /Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy/src/components/GameInvitationResponseForm/GameInvitationResponseForm.stories.tsx has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text: The mock players all have the same name "Gary Player" (they inherit from `defaultPlayer`), with the first player having id=1. I'll select by clicking the first option with that label:

content.text:
All 95 test files and 162 tests passed. The fix replaces `userEvent.selectOptions` (which only works with native `<select>` elements) with the Mantine combobox interaction pattern:

1. **`GameInvitationResponseForm.stories.tsx`**: click the combobox input, find the listbox that appears in `document.body`, then click the `'Yes'` option by role.
2. **`NewPlayerForm.stories.tsx`**: same approach — click the combobox, find the listbox, then click `options[1]` (index 0 is "(Nobody)", index 1 is the first real player, matching the original value `'1'`).
